The user wants me to identify the time segments where Mel Rojas JR is visible in the video.

1.  **Analyze the video:**
    *   00:00 - 01:06: The video shows a couple on a couch and then a graphic of the "Equipo Todos Estrellas" (All-Star Team) for the 2023 Caribbean Series. Mel Rojas JR is listed on the graphic, but he is not visually present as a person.
    *   01:06 - 01:21: The scene shifts to a baseball game. A batter wearing a blue jersey with "DOM" and number 26 is at the plate. The on-screen graphic identifies him as "26 M. ROJAS JR". He is batting.
    *   01:21 - 01:31: The camera cuts to a close-up of Mel Rojas JR walking back to the dugout after striking out. He is clearly visible, wearing jersey number 26.
    *   01:31 - 01:45: The camera cuts back to the pitcher and batter. Mel Rojas JR is at the plate again.
    *   01:45 - 01:52: Mel Rojas JR hits the ball and runs to first base. He is visible running.
    *   01:52 - 02:06: The camera focuses on the pitcher (Yonny Gomes, #61) and then pans out to the stadium. Mel Rojas JR is no longer the focus and is not clearly visible in the wide shot.
    *   02:06 - 02:32: The video shows wide shots of the stadium and then back to the pitcher and a new batter (#39). Mel Rojas JR is not visible.

2.  **Define segments:**
    *   Segment 1: 01:06 to 01:21 (Batting)
    *   Segment 2: 01:21 to 01:31 (Walking to dugout)
    *   Segment 3: 01:31 to 01:45 (Batting again)
    *   Segment 4: 01:45 to 01:52 (Running to first base)

    Since these segments are continuous or very close with no significant break in his presence on screen (the cuts are just different angles of the same at-bat sequence), I can combine them into one continuous segment from his first appearance to his last clear appearance.

    Start time: 01:06
    End time: 01:52

3.  **Check constraints:**
    *   Face visible: Yes, in several shots.
    *   Region: Center/Right of the frame mostly.
    *   Presence: Full body, upper body.

4.  **Format output:**

    *   End time: 112.0 seconds (01:52)
